overview: A microservices architecture for leave management consisting of a React frontend (leave-web-app) that communicates with two backend Go services: leave-service handles leave requests and approvals, while employee-service manages employee data and leave balances. The services expose RESTful APIs and run as independent Docker containers on Kubernetes.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests with date range and leave type
Managers must be able to view pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests
Employees must be able to view status of their submitted requests (pending, approved, rejected)
All users must be able to check their remaining leave balance
System must validate leave requests against available balance
System must track leave types (annual, sick, etc.)
All services must expose health check endpoints

components.name: leave-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Manages leave requests, approvals, and status tracking
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /leave-requests:
    post:
      summary: Submit a new leave request
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - employeeId
                - startDate
                - endDate
                - leaveType
              properties:
                employeeId:
                  type: string
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [annual, sick, personal, unpaid]
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input or insufficient balance
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
    get:
      summary: Get leave requests for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
  /leave-requests/{requestId}:
    get:
      summary: Get a specific leave request
      parameters:
        - name: requestId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Request not found
  /leave-requests/{requestId}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: requestId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comments:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Manager not authorized
        '404':
          description: Request not found
  /leave-requests/{requestId}/reject:
    post:
      summary: Reject a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: requestId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
                - reason
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request rejected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '403':
          description: Manager not authorized
        '404':
          description: Request not found
  /leave-requests/pending:
    get:
      summary: Get pending requests for a manager
      parameters:
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of pending requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        managerId:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        leaveType:
          type: string
          enum: [annual, sick, personal, unpaid]
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        reason:
          type: string
        managerComments:
          type: string
        daysRequested:
          type: number
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go service using net/http that listens on port 9090. Use an in-memory data structure (map or slice) to store leave requests with fields: id (UUID), employeeId, managerId, startDate, endDate, leaveType (annual/sick/personal/unpaid), status (pending/approved/rejected), reason, managerComments, daysRequested (calculated from date range), createdAt, updatedAt. When a leave request is submitted via POST /leave-requests, generate a UUID, calculate the number of days between startDate and endDate (excluding weekends), fetch the employee's manager ID and current leave balance by calling GET http://employee-service:9090/employees/{employeeId}, verify the employee has sufficient balance for the requested leave type, set status to pending, store the request, and return 201 with the created request or 400 if validation fails. For GET /leave-requests?employeeId={id}, filter and return all requests for that employee. For GET /leave-requests/{requestId}, return the specific request or 404 if not found. For POST /leave-requests/{requestId}/approve, verify the managerId in the request body matches the request's managerId by calling GET http://employee-service:9090/employees/{employeeId} to check manager relationship, update status to approved, add managerComments, set updatedAt, then call POST http://employee-service:9090/employees/{employeeId}/deduct-leave with leaveType and daysRequested to update the employee's balance, return 200 with updated request or 403 if manager unauthorized. For POST /leave-requests/{requestId}/reject, perform same manager verification, update status to rejected, add reason to managerComments, return 200 or 403. For GET /leave-requests/pending?managerId={id}, call GET http://employee-service:9090/employees?managerId={id} to get all direct reports, filter requests where status is pending and employeeId matches a direct report, return the list. Implement GET /health returning {"status":"ok"} with 200. Use encoding/json for JSON marshaling. Handle errors gracefully with appropriate HTTP status codes. Use time.Parse for date parsing in YYYY-MM-DD format. Calculate business days excluding weekends. Use UUIDs from github.com/google/uuid package. Log all HTTP requests and errors to stdout.

components.name: employee-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Employee Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Manages employee data, leave balances, and organizational structure
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /employees/{employeeId}:
    get:
      summary: Get employee details including leave balance
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Employee details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
  /employees:
    get:
      summary: Get employees by manager
      parameters:
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of employees
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
    post:
      summary: Create a new employee
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - id
                - name
                - email
              properties:
                id:
                  type: string
                name:
                  type: string
                email:
                  type: string
                managerId:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Employee created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
  /employees/{employeeId}/leave-balance:
    get:
      summary: Get leave balance for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveBalance'
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
  /employees/{employeeId}/deduct-leave:
    post:
      summary: Deduct leave days from employee balance
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - leaveType
                - days
              properties:
                leaveType:
                  type: string
                  enum: [annual, sick, personal, unpaid]
                days:
                  type: number
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveBalance'
        '400':
          description: Insufficient balance
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
components:
  schemas:
    Employee:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        managerId:
          type: string
        leaveBalance:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveBalance'
    LeaveBalance:
      type: object
      properties:
        annual:
          type: number
        sick:
          type: number
        personal:
          type: number
        unpaid:
          type: number
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go service using net/http that listens on port 9090. Use an in-memory map to store employee records with fields: id (string), name, email, managerId (nullable string), and leaveBalance (struct with annual, sick, personal, unpaid as float64). On startup, seed the data store with at least 5 sample employees: create 2 managers (managerId is empty/nil) and 3 employees reporting to those managers. Initialize leave balances with annual=20, sick=10, personal=5, unpaid=0 for all employees. Implement GET /employees/{employeeId} to return the full employee object including nested leaveBalance or 404 if not found. Implement GET /employees?managerId={id} to filter and return employees where managerId matches the query parameter; if no managerId query param is provided, return all employees. Implement POST /employees to create a new employee with provided id, name, email, managerId (optional), initialize default leave balances (annual=20, sick=10, personal=5, unpaid=0), store in map, return 201 with created employee. Implement GET /employees/{employeeId}/leave-balance to return only the leaveBalance object for that employee or 404. Implement POST /employees/{employeeId}/deduct-leave to subtract the specified days from the appropriate leave type balance; validate that sufficient balance exists before deducting (return 400 if insufficient), update the balance, return 200 with updated leaveBalance or 404 if employee not found. Unpaid leave type should always succeed as it has no limit. Implement GET /health returning {"status":"ok"} with 200. Use encoding/json for all JSON operations. Log all requests to stdout. Handle concurrent access safely using sync.RWMutex around the employee map. Return proper HTTP status codes for all error cases.

components.name: leave-web-app
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Web App
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend application for leave management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Create a React single-page application using TypeScript, Vite, and React Router. Implement a simple authentication simulation using localStorage to store currentUserId and role (employee or manager). Create a login page where users select their identity from a dropdown populated by calling GET http://employee-service:9090/employees, storing the selected employeeId in localStorage. Implement four main pages: (1) Dashboard showing current user's name, role, and leave balance fetched from GET http://employee-service:9090/employees/{employeeId}/leave-balance; (2) My Requests page for employees showing their leave requests from GET http://leave-service:9090/leave-requests?employeeId={id} with status badges (pending=yellow, approved=green, rejected=red); (3) Submit Request page with a form containing date pickers for start/end dates, dropdown for leave type (annual/sick/personal/unpaid), textarea for reason, submit button that POSTs to http://leave-service:9090/leave-requests and redirects to My Requests on success, shows error message if insufficient balance; (4) Pending Approvals page for managers showing pending requests from GET http://leave-service:9090/leave-requests/pending?managerId={id} with Approve and Reject buttons, clicking Approve POSTs to http://leave-service:9090/leave-requests/{requestId}/approve with managerId and optional comments, clicking Reject shows a modal to collect rejection reason then POSTs to http://leave-service:9090/leave-requests/{requestId}/reject. Use React hooks (useState, useEffect) for state management. Use fetch API for all HTTP calls with error handling. Style with Tailwind CSS or basic CSS for a clean, professional look. Show loading spinners during API calls. Display error messages in red text. Navigation bar should show Dashboard, My Requests, Submit Request for all users, plus Pending Approvals for managers only. Implement GET /health endpoint in the production server (express or serve static) returning {"status":"ok"} with 200. Use environment variables for API base URLs with defaults to http://leave-service:9090 and http://employee-service:9090. Build the app with npm run build. Serve static files on port 3000. Dockerfile should use node:18-alpine, run npm install, npm run build, and serve with npx serve -s dist -l 3000. Include package.json with dependencies: react, react-dom, react-router-dom, typescript, vite, @types/react, @types/react-dom. Add a simple README explaining how to run the app.
